meta.title: UK Job Market Visualizer
meta.source_name: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey
meta.source_detail: Employment by 4-digit SOC 2020 occupation and 1-digit industry, with ASHE pay and Nomis regional overlays
notes: Uses the 2024 reported employment count for each 4-digit SOC occupation.
Median Pay uses ASHE Table 14 annual gross pay for 2024 at 4-digit SOC 2020 level.

Regional Employment maps each 4-digit tile to its parent 3-digit SOC group using Nomis APS counts for Jan 2024-Dec 2024. Colour shows how over- or under-represented that parent group is in the selected region relative to the UK mix.
Occupation totals are lower-bound sums of reported industry cells; ONS suppressed '*' cells are excluded from the total.
AI exposure scores are based on the 2023 paper 'The Future of Skills: Employment in 2030' by Pearson and Nesta.

occupations.title: Chief executives and senior officials
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role is predominantly digital, involving strategic planning, policy analysis, and communication, all areas where AI is rapidly improving. AI can automate data analysis, policy drafting, and communication, significantly increasing productivity and reshaping the occupation.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Elected officers and representatives
occupations.exposure_rationale: Elected representatives primarily engage in policy formulation, legislative drafting, and data analysis—tasks that are fully digital and increasingly automated by AI. AI tools can generate draft legislation, analyze legislative impacts, and support policy recommendations, making the occupation highly susceptible to AI-driven restructuring.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Production managers and directors in manufacturing
occupations.exposure_rationale: Production managers and directors perform predominantly digital, knowledge-based tasks such as planning, cost analysis, and reporting, which are well-suited to AI automation. While they still oversee physical production lines, the core work is increasingly AI-driven, leading to high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Production managers and directors in construction
occupations.exposure_rationale: Production managers and directors in construction perform knowledge work such as planning, cost estimation, and coordination, which are increasingly aided by AI tools. However, the role still requires physical presence on construction sites and real-time human judgment, limiting AI's impact to a moderate degree.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Production managers and directors in mining and energy
occupations.exposure_rationale: Production managers and directors primarily coordinate complex, physical operations and ensure regulatory compliance, which involve substantial human judgment and real‑time interaction. AI can assist with data analysis, scheduling, and predictive maintenance, but the core role remains largely human‑driven and not fully digital.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Financial managers and directors
occupations.exposure_rationale: Financial managers and directors perform predominantly digital, knowledge-based tasks such as strategic planning, financial analysis, and compliance monitoring, all of which are increasingly automated by AI. The job's core work is fully digital, with no physical component, placing it in the high‑exposure range.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Marketing, sales and advertising directors
occupations.exposure_rationale: Marketing, sales and advertising directors perform predominantly digital, knowledge-based tasks such as strategy planning, market analysis, and campaign design, all of which are increasingly automated by AI. Their work is largely virtual, with no physical component, placing them in a high‑exposure zone where AI can significantly augment or replace routine functions.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Public relations and communications directors
occupations.exposure_rationale: Public relations and communications directors perform predominantly digital, knowledge-based tasks such as media strategy, campaign planning, and message distribution, all of which are increasingly automated by AI tools. The job's core output is digital and can be largely performed remotely, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven restructuring.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Purchasing managers and directors
occupations.exposure_rationale: Purchasing managers and directors perform predominantly digital, knowledge‑based tasks such as policy development, contract negotiation, supplier evaluation, and research, all of which are increasingly automated by AI. The role can be performed remotely, and AI tools are already enhancing productivity, indicating very high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Charitable organisation managers and directors
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role is predominantly knowledge-based, involving policy formulation, fundraising coordination, communications, and digital reporting, all of which are increasingly automated by AI. While some physical presence may be needed for meetings, the core work is digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven efficiency gains.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Human resource managers and directors
occupations.exposure_rationale: HR managers and directors work primarily with digital HR systems, data analytics, and policy development, all areas where AI is rapidly improving. AI can automate recruitment, training design, performance monitoring, and compliance analysis, significantly reshaping the job.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Information technology directors
occupations.exposure_rationale: Information technology directors perform predominantly digital, knowledge‑based work that involves planning, strategy, and project coordination, all of which are increasingly automated by AI. Their role is largely performed on computers, with AI tools already enhancing productivity, indicating very high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Functional managers and directors n.e.c.
occupations.exposure_rationale: Functional managers and directors perform predominantly digital, knowledge‑intensive tasks such as policy formulation, resource planning, communication, and reporting, which are core to AI’s rapid advancement. Their work is largely remote and can be fully executed on computers, creating a high ceiling for AI impact.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Directors in logistics, warehousing and transport
occupations.minor_group_label: Directors in Logistics, Warehousing and Transport
occupations.exposure_rationale: Directors in logistics, warehousing and transport perform knowledge work involving data analysis, planning, and coordination, which can be augmented by AI tools. However, the role requires physical presence at transport hubs, real-time human interaction, and judgment in unpredictable environments, limiting full automation. This moderate mix of digital and physical tasks places it in the 4‑5 range.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Managers and directors in retail and wholesale
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Directors in Retail and Wholesale
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role is predominantly knowledge-based, involving planning, coordination, and supervising staff, all of which can be performed remotely via digital tools. AI can automate scheduling, performance analytics, inventory forecasting, and financial reporting, significantly boosting productivity. The job's core functions are digital and AI is already enhancing them, indicating very high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Officers in armed forces
occupations.exposure_rationale: Officers in armed forces perform predominantly knowledge‑based, strategic planning and policy‑advice tasks that are increasingly digital, so AI can assist with analysis and coordination. However, many duties require real‑time physical presence, command authority, and unpredictable field operations, limiting AI's direct impact on core work.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Senior police officers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Senior police officers perform knowledge-based tasks such as planning, coordinating, and liaising that are increasingly digitized and amenable to AI assistance, especially in data analysis and communication. However, their work still requires real-time human presence, judgment, and interpersonal interaction, limiting full automation.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Senior officers in fire, ambulance, prison and related servi
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role is primarily physical and emergency‑response oriented, requiring real‑time human presence and coordination in unpredictable environments. AI may assist with reporting or staffing planning, but the core work cannot be automated, resulting in low AI exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Health services and public health managers and directors
occupations.minor_group_label: Health and Social Services Managers and Directors
occupations.exposure_rationale: These managers perform knowledge-intensive tasks such as policy implementation, data analysis, and coordination, which are increasingly aided by AI tools. However, their work still requires human judgment, interpersonal interaction, and physical presence in healthcare settings, limiting AI's full automation potential.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Social services managers and directors
occupations.minor_group_label: Health and Social Services Managers and Directors
occupations.exposure_rationale: Social services managers and directors perform knowledge‑intensive tasks such as policy analysis, staffing planning, and performance monitoring, which can be augmented by AI tools. However, their core work involves human judgment, inter‑agency liaison, and managing complex, non‑digital service delivery, limiting AI’s transformative impact.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Managers and proprietors in agriculture and horticulture
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Agriculture Related Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role is primarily knowledge-based, involving planning, financial analysis, and decision‑making that can be performed digitally, making it susceptible to AI tools that automate forecasting, supply ordering, and marketing. However, managers often need to be on‑site to oversee physical operations, limiting full automation.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Managers and proprietors in forestry, fishing and related se
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Agriculture Related Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role is predominantly knowledge-based with planning, coordination, and decision-making, allowing AI to assist with data analysis and scheduling, but core tasks involve physical oversight of forest and fish operations, requiring human presence and judgment. This moderate mix of physical and managerial duties places it in the 4-5 range.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Hotel and accommodation managers and proprietors
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Hospitality and Leisure Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: Hotel managers perform extensive data analysis, financial planning, staffing, and operational coordination, all digital tasks that AI can automate. Their work is largely knowledge-based and increasingly digital, leading to high AI exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Restaurant and catering establishment managers and proprieto
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Hospitality and Leisure Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role is predominantly knowledge-based, involving planning, budgeting, and staff coordination, which are areas where AI can assist with inventory, scheduling, and menu optimization. However, the job requires physical presence in kitchens and dining areas, maintaining hygiene, and real-time human interaction, limiting AI's impact to a moderate level.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Publicans and managers of licensed premises
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Hospitality and Leisure Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role combines knowledge work (inventory, compliance, financial management) with physical presence and supervision of staff, limiting AI's direct impact on core duties. AI can assist with scheduling, inventory tracking, and compliance monitoring, but the essential human interaction and on‑site management remain central.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Leisure and sports managers
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Hospitality and Leisure Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: Leisure and sports managers perform predominantly knowledge‑based, digital tasks such as scheduling, trend analysis, financial planning, and marketing that can be performed remotely. AI tools are already useful for these activities, and the job's core functions are increasingly digitised, indicating high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Travel agency managers and proprietors
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Hospitality and Leisure Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: Travel agency managers and proprietors perform predominantly digital, knowledge-based tasks such as planning itineraries, advising clients, and coordinating bookings, all of which can be augmented or automated by AI tools. The job is largely remote and information‑processing focused, placing it in the high‑exposure range.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Health care practice managers
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Health and Care Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: Healthcare practice managers perform predominantly digital, knowledge-based tasks such as scheduling, budgeting, staff training, and data coordination, all of which are increasingly automated by AI. The role can be fully executed remotely, and AI tools are already enhancing productivity, indicating very high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Residential, day and domiciliary care managers and proprieto
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Health and Care Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role is primarily knowledge-based, involving planning, staffing, and coordination, which are areas where AI can assist with data analysis and scheduling. However, core tasks such as human interaction, trust-building, and physical service delivery remain essential, limiting AI's transformative impact.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Early education and childcare services proprietors
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Health and Care Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role is primarily knowledge-based, involving planning, staffing, and compliance monitoring, which can be augmented by AI tools for scheduling and data analysis. However, core responsibilities still require physical presence and human interaction with children and parents, limiting AI's impact to moderate levels.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Managers in transport and distribution
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers in Logistics, Warehousing and Transport
occupations.exposure_rationale: Managers in transport and distribution perform predominantly knowledge‑based tasks such as planning, coordination, and data analysis, which are increasingly automated by AI tools for route optimization, predictive maintenance, and demand forecasting. While AI can handle many routine aspects, the role still requires human judgment, oversight, and interaction with physical logistics, placing it in the high‑exposure range.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Managers in storage and warehousing
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers in Logistics, Warehousing and Transport
occupations.exposure_rationale: Managers in storage and warehousing perform knowledge‑intensive tasks such as planning, policy development, and reporting that are increasingly digitised and amenable to AI assistance (e.g., inventory forecasting, space optimisation). While some physical presence is still required, the core work is digital and AI tools are already enhancing productivity, indicating high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Managers in logistics
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers in Logistics, Warehousing and Transport
occupations.exposure_rationale: Logistics managers perform predominantly digital, knowledge-based tasks such as supply‑chain planning, data analysis, and regulatory compliance, which are core domains where AI is rapidly improving. Their work can be fully executed remotely, and AI tools already assist in optimization and forecasting, indicating very high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Property, housing and estate managers
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Other Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role is predominantly knowledge-based, involving planning, financial management, legal advice, and digital record-keeping, all of which are increasingly automated by AI. While some physical maintenance tasks remain, the core decision-making and coordination are digital and AI‑driven, placing it in the high‑exposure range.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Garage managers and proprietors
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Other Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: Garage managers and proprietors perform knowledge‑intensive tasks such as staffing planning, compliance checks, and record‑keeping that can be digitized and automated with AI. While core work remains human‑driven and involves physical presence, AI tools are already enhancing productivity, indicating high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Hairdressing and beauty salon managers and proprietors
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Other Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role is primarily physical and interpersonal, involving manual hair treatments and equipment maintenance, which are not easily automated. AI can assist with scheduling, inventory, and client record-keeping, but these are peripheral tasks. Hence, moderate exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Waste disposal and environmental services managers
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Other Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role is primarily managerial and involves planning, staffing, compliance, and community liaison, which are knowledge‑intensive and can be augmented by AI tools for data analysis and predictive scheduling. However, core tasks still require physical presence for equipment maintenance and waste handling, limiting AI's impact to moderate levels.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Managers and directors in the creative industries
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Other Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: Managers and directors in creative industries perform predominantly digital, knowledge-based tasks such as planning, client liaison, trend analysis, and creative direction, all of which are increasingly automated by AI tools. Their work is fully digital and relies on AI for content generation, market insights, and operational efficiency, leading to high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Betting shop and gambling establishment managers
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Other Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: Betting shop managers perform knowledge work such as budgeting, compliance monitoring, and staff coordination, which can be augmented by AI tools. However, their role heavily involves physical presence, regulatory oversight, and direct customer interaction, limiting AI's impact on core tasks.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Hire services managers and proprietors
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Other Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role is predominantly knowledge‑based and can be performed remotely, involving planning, coordination, client liaison and compliance—areas where AI tools are already enhancing productivity. The digital nature of the work places it in a high‑exposure category.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Directors in consultancy services
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Other Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: Directors in consultancy services perform predominantly digital, knowledge‑intensive tasks such as strategic planning, client liaison, and market analysis, all of which are increasingly automated by AI. The role can be performed remotely, and AI tools are already enhancing productivity, indicating very high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Managers and proprietors in other services n.e.c.
occupations.minor_group_label: Managers and Proprietors in Other Services
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role is predominantly knowledge‑based, involving planning, staffing, finance and service coordination that are increasingly digitised. AI tools can automate many of these tasks, such as predictive staffing and inventory optimisation, making the occupation highly susceptible to AI‑driven restructuring.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Chemical scientists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Chemical scientists perform knowledge-intensive tasks such as data analysis and experimental design that are increasingly automated by AI, but their work also requires physical lab work and equipment operation, limiting full AI substitution. This mix places the occupation in the high exposure range.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Biological scientists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Biological scientists perform largely knowledge‑intensive tasks such as data analysis, modeling, and communication that are increasingly digitized and amenable to AI assistance. While many activities still involve fieldwork or lab work, the core scientific work is digital and can be augmented by AI, leading to high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Biochemists and biomedical scientists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Biochemists and biomedical scientists perform largely knowledge-intensive tasks such as analyzing biological samples, diagnosing diseases, and conducting research, which are increasingly supported by AI tools for data analysis and predictive modeling. However, the job requires physical presence in laboratories and hands‑on experimentation, limiting AI's ability to replace the core work entirely, resulting in moderate exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Physical scientists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Physical scientists rely heavily on digital data analysis, mathematical modeling, and forecasting, which are core AI domains. While some fieldwork remains, the majority of tasks are increasingly automated, indicating very high AI exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Social and humanities scientists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Social and humanities scientists perform predominantly digital research, data analysis, and communication tasks that are increasingly automated by AI, such as literature review, quantitative analysis, and language pattern detection. Their work is fully digital and AI tools are already enhancing productivity, leading to high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Natural and social science professionals n.e.c.
occupations.exposure_rationale: The occupation is predominantly knowledge‑based, involving data analysis, research design, and communication, all of which are increasingly automated by AI. While some physical lab work remains, the core tasks are digital and can be substantially augmented or replaced by AI tools.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Civil engineers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Civil engineers perform predominantly knowledge‑based tasks such as design, analysis, and planning, which are increasingly digital and amenable to AI assistance. However, their work also requires physical presence for site inspections and construction oversight, limiting full automation. This mix of digital knowledge work and physical constraints places them in the moderate‑to‑high exposure range.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Mechanical engineers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Mechanical engineers perform knowledge-intensive tasks such as design, research, and analysis that are increasingly aided by AI tools for optimization and simulation. However, the core work involves physical systems, safety standards, and real-world constraints that limit AI's direct impact, resulting in moderate exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Electrical engineers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Electrical engineers perform predominantly digital tasks such as design, control system monitoring, and system optimization, all of which are increasingly automated by AI. The job's core work is digital and AI can significantly augment or replace routine aspects, leading to high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Electronics engineers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Electronics engineers primarily work with digital design, analysis, and testing of electronic systems, which are core domains where AI is rapidly advancing. Their tasks involve research, data processing, and system optimization, all of which can be augmented or automated by AI, leading to high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Production and process engineers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Production and process engineers perform predominantly digital, knowledge‑intensive tasks such as process design, optimization, and data analysis, all of which are increasingly automated by AI. Their work is largely computer‑based, with AI tools already enhancing efficiency and potentially replacing routine analytical duties.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Aerospace engineers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Aerospace engineers primarily engage in digital design, analysis, and research tasks that are increasingly automated by AI tools. Their work is largely computer‑based, involving simulations, data processing, and design optimization, which are core domains where AI is rapidly advancing, leading to high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Engineering project managers and project engineers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Engineering project managers and project engineers perform largely digital tasks such as budgeting, scheduling, and technical documentation, which are susceptible to AI assistance. However, they also require physical inspection and on‑site monitoring, limiting full automation. This mix places them in the moderate exposure range.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Engineering professionals n.e.c.
occupations.exposure_rationale: Engineering professionals perform largely knowledge‑based tasks such as research, design, and technical consulting that are increasingly digital and amenable to AI assistance. While some physical work remains, the core activities are in AI‑enhanceable domains, indicating high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: IT project managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: IT project managers perform predominantly digital, knowledge-based tasks such as planning, coordination, and reporting, which are increasingly automated by AI tools. While AI can assist with scheduling and risk analysis, the core role of managing human teams and client communication remains, indicating very high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: IT managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: IT managers perform predominantly knowledge-based tasks such as planning, coordinating, and supervising IT services, which are increasingly supported by AI tools for data analysis and automation. While AI can enhance efficiency, the core work still requires human judgment, relationship management, and oversight, placing it in the high exposure range.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: IT business analysts, architects and systems designers
occupations.exposure_rationale: The occupation is fundamentally digital, involving analysis, design, and implementation of IT systems, all of which are increasingly automated by AI. AI tools can perform routine data analysis, feasibility studies, and system design, dramatically increasing productivity and reducing the need for human labor.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Programmers and software development professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: The occupation is entirely digital, with core tasks such as writing code, designing software, and creating user interfaces that are increasingly automated by AI. AI tools can generate code, optimize designs, and perform testing, making the job highly susceptible to AI-driven restructuring.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Cyber security professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: Cyber security professionals work primarily with digital systems, using software to analyze threats and respond to breaches, which are core knowledge tasks. AI is already enhancing threat detection and automation, indicating high exposure, though some physical or on-site work may limit full automation.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: IT quality and testing professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: IT quality and testing professionals work primarily with digital software, systems, and games, making the job highly amenable to AI automation of test case generation, defect prediction, and analysis. However, they still require human judgment for complex issues and strategic recommendations, resulting in moderate to high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: IT network professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: IT network professionals perform predominantly digital tasks such as monitoring, troubleshooting, and security management, which are increasingly automated by AI tools. Their work is knowledge‑intensive and can be done remotely, placing them in a high‑exposure category where AI will significantly reshape the job.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Information technology professionals n.e.c.
occupations.exposure_rationale: The occupation is fundamentally digital, involving software, hardware, network management, and website development, all of which are rapidly being automated or augmented by AI. This high digital nature and the extensive use of AI tools for design, testing, and monitoring give it very high exposure, approaching the maximum.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Web design professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: Web design is fundamentally digital, involving creating and maintaining websites and mobile apps using multimedia techniques. AI tools are already augmenting design processes, and the trajectory is steep, indicating very high exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Graphic and multimedia designers
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role is fundamentally digital, involving design, multimedia, and 3D creation, all of which are increasingly augmented by AI tools that can generate assets, automate repetitive tasks, and enhance creativity. AI is already reshaping workflows, making the job more productive and potentially reducing the need for certain manual steps.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Conservation professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: Conservation professionals combine knowledge work such as policy, research, and communication with fieldwork that requires physical presence. AI can assist with data analysis, environmental monitoring, and drafting reports, but the core tasks involve hands‑on habitat management and real‑time human interaction, limiting AI's impact to a moderate level.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Environment professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: Environment professionals perform largely knowledge‑based tasks such as data analysis, policy advice, and communication, which are increasingly digitized and amenable to AI assistance. However, the occupation also requires fieldwork and physical site visits, limiting full automation. This mix places it in the moderate‑to‑high exposure range.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Research and development (R&D) managers
occupations.minor_group_label: Research and Development (R&D) and Other Research Professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: R&D managers perform predominantly knowledge‑based, digital tasks such as research methodology design, data analysis, and strategic planning, all of which are increasingly automated by AI. Their work is largely performed from a computer, with minimal physical presence, placing them in the high‑exposure range.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Other researchers, unspecified discipline
occupations.minor_group_label: Research and Development (R&D) and Other Research Professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: The occupation is primarily digital, involving research, analysis, writing, and presentation tasks that are fully computer-based. AI can automate literature reviews, data analysis, and report generation, significantly increasing productivity. This places it in the high exposure range.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Generalist medical practitioners
occupations.exposure_rationale: Generalist medical practitioners perform predominantly knowledge-based tasks such as diagnosis, treatment planning, and research, all of which are digital and increasingly augmented by AI tools. AI can assist in interpreting test results, recommending treatments, and supporting research, leading to substantial productivity gains and potential restructuring of the role.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Specialist medical practitioners
occupations.exposure_rationale: Specialist medical practitioners perform predominantly digital tasks such as diagnosis, treatment planning, research, and education, all of which are increasingly automated by AI. AI tools can already assist in image analysis, diagnostic support, and research analysis, leading to substantial productivity gains and potential workforce restructuring.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Physiotherapists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Physiotherapists perform hands‑on clinical work that is largely physical and requires real‑time human interaction, limiting AI's direct impact on core tasks. However, AI can assist with medical record analysis, treatment planning, and progress monitoring, giving the job moderate exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Occupational therapists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Occupational therapists perform knowledge‑intensive tasks such as assessment, treatment planning, and counseling, which are increasingly supported by AI tools for data analysis and remote monitoring. However, the job still requires physical presence, interpersonal interaction, and nuanced human judgment, limiting AI's ability to replace core activities.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Speech and language therapists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Speech and language therapists perform knowledge‑intensive clinical work that requires human judgment, patient interaction, and ethical decision‑making, limiting full AI replacement. However, AI tools are increasingly used for assessment, diagnosis, and treatment planning, creating moderate exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Psychotherapists and cognitive behaviour therapists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Psychotherapists and cognitive behavioural therapists primarily deliver human‑centered, face‑to‑face or video sessions, so AI cannot replace the core therapeutic interaction. However, AI tools are increasingly used for assessment, note‑taking, and remote monitoring, giving the job moderate exposure.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Clinical psychologists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Clinical psychologists perform knowledge-intensive tasks such as assessments, treatment planning, and report writing that are increasingly aided by AI tools, raising exposure to 5. However, the core work remains highly interpersonal and requires human judgment, limiting the ceiling to a moderate level rather than very high.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Other psychologists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Psychologists perform many digital tasks such as test administration, data analysis, and therapy planning that are increasingly automatable with AI. occupations.exposure_rationale: The occupation involves real-time physical presence, combat duties, and guarding in unpredictable environments, which cannot be automated by AI. AI has no meaningful impact on core tasks.

occupations.title: Police officers (sergeant and below)
occupations.exposure_rationale: Police work is primarily physical and real-time, involving patrols, arrests, and direct interaction with the public, which AI cannot replace. While AI may assist with data analysis or predictive tools, the core job remains hands‑on and interpersonal.
occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Fire service officers (watch manager and below)
occupations.exposure_rationale: Fire service officers perform predominantly physical, real-time tasks such as inspections, emergency response, equipment operation, and rescues that require immediate human presence and cannot be automated. AI has minimal impact on these core duties.

occupations.title: Prison service officers (below principal officer)
occupations.exposure_rationale: The occupation is primarily physical and requires real‑time human presence for security and supervision, limiting AI's direct impact. AI may assist with peripheral tasks like surveillance data, but core duties remain manual and interpersonal.

occupations.title: Protective service associate professionals n.e.c.

occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Artists

occupations.source: Office for National Statistics Annual Population Survey

occupations.title: Actors, entertainers and presenters
